title: Floral, Velvety Venezulan Oats Pilaf
description: A sweet and comforting brunch dish inspired by the flavors of Venezuela, featuring a floral and velvety porridge oats pilaf made with roasted plantains, aromatic spices, and a touch of honey.

ingredients.name: Rolled Oats
ingredients.notes: Steel-cut or old-fashioned oats can be used for a slightly chewier texture.

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Full-fat coconut milk adds richness and creaminess.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for sweetness level.

instructions: Peel and slice the plantains into 1-inch rounds. Place the plantain slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and drizzle with a tablespoon of oil.
Roast the plantains in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and caramelized.
In a large saucepan, combine the oats, coconut milk, honey, cinnamon, ginger, and salt. Stir to combine.
Add the roasted plantains and unsweetened almond milk to the saucepan. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the oats have absorbed most of the liquid and the mixture has thickened.
Reduce heat to low and stir in 1 tablespoon of honey. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the mixture has reached the desired consistency.
Serve the pilaf warm, garnished with additional honey and plantain slices if desired.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
